title: swainsona bracteata
intro: Swainsona bracteata, also known as the sticky hopbush or bracted spiny pea, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It is a shrub that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is known for its sticky hairs and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used traditionally by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Swainsona bracteata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or poultice for its health benefits, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
